reviews.text:
I would not go back - shallow approach .. fees that exceed any insurance premium for no reason .. silly chats
To top up the second consultation is over the phone max on 2 min charged at the rate of £250
Give us a break

I have waited a while to write this review for various reasons. However, given the potential negative impact on many other women who might have the same misfortune of coming across Dr Sarah Glynne, I thought I must follow up with a review.

I went to see Sarah Glynne following some hormonal issues post covid. During the appointment, I was told that I was in "early perimenopause", despite having impeccable blood work and no other evidence outside of a range of symptoms (some of which overlap with perimenopause, but can also be seen in Long covid). She then suggested I would need to be on HRT until age 51 (I am 30). She told me that she would keep upping my dose up to a maximum, until I find relief from my symptoms. I was also told that lifestyle adjustments cannot balance hormones and have no impact. She told me that covid damages the ovaries and that they only "bounce back in younger women".

Naturally, I left the appointment very demoralised to say the least.

I then consulted a highly regarded and, unlike Sarah Glynne, actually qualified Gynaecologist, to get a second opinion. Long story short and to paraphrase, he said that her recommendations and assessment were NONSENSE. There is no evidence that I am in perimenopause and also no evidence of Long covid having lasting impact on ovaries. He also told me that my symptoms would be different in frequency and cadence if it was indeed perimenopause. And that my symptoms were more likely a result of dysregulation post covid, which can also impact hormonal balance (but this is NOT perimenopause). He prescribed me a different treatment option which was much more sensible, and also recommended lifestyle changes as he believes these DO make a big difference. And indeed they have done. Sarah Glynne was entirely wrong in her assessment and her recommendations were dangerous.

I am writing this because:

- A. I want my money back for the £300 consultation which was not only a waste of money but also caused more harm than good (especially to my mental health). I am also grateful I followed my gut and did NOT go on HRT given the risks.

- B. I do not want other poor young women to come across Sarah Glynne and have the misfortune to be told they are perimenopausal when this is NOT the case.

To conclude, I believe women struggling with post covid issues with hormones consult a qualified Gynaecologist before making such conclusions about their health, and taking actions that can cause serious harm.

Sarah Glynne has clearly ignored the Hippocratic oath of "first do no harm", as harm she did cause. I do hope Claire Mellon & Associates offer reasonable compensation following this message, as I do not want to escalate things further.

I did have the most traumatic experience I ever had at the gynaecologist at this clinic. I wouldn't want to go into too much details, but the routine examination was so painful, that I couldn't sleep two nights after and had to take pain killers. The Doctor I saw was not Claire Mellon though, so probably it would have been different. I would not recommend seeing this person to anyone, and having her in a clinic puts its reputation at a big risk.

I was hoping to get some good advice from a prestigious, but I was left disappointed and shocked by the comment the Gynaecologist Emma Kirk
made. …
Appointments cancelled at short notice, poor appointment availability, particularly if you’re working full time, and curt staff. Would not recommend.
